title: lavandula canariensis subsp. gomerensis
intro: Lavandula canariensis subsp. gomerensis, also known as the tree lavender, is a subspecies of the Canarian lavender plant, native to the Canary Islands. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of tree lavender include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting better sleep, and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders. Tree lavender can be used in various herbal preparations, such as essential oils, teas, and topical creams, making it a versatile natural remedy for various health concerns.
